title: ALIAS statement in query does not return correct cols for distributed table
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When using ALIAS fields in distributed queries with sorting, data from column does not match.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE DATABASE test ON CLUSTER '{cluster}';
CREATE TABLE test.t_local ON CLUSTER '{cluster}' (
id String,
c1 String,
c2 String,
d1 String ALIAS concat(c1, '-d1'),
d2 String ALIAS concat(c2, '-d2')
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE test.t ON CLUSTER '{cluster}'
AS test.t_local
ENGINE = Distributed('{cluster}', 'test', t_local, rand());
INSERT INTO test.t (id, c1, c2) VALUES
('1', 'id1-1', 'id2-1'),
('2', 'id1-2', 'id2-2'),
('3', 'id1-1', 'id2-1'),
('4', 'id1-2', 'id2-2');
SELECT id, d1, d2 FROM test.t;
SELECT id, d1, d2 FROM test.t ORDER BY c1;
DROP DATABASE test ON CLUSTER '{cluster}'
```
### Expected behavior
select 1 ( no order )
```
в”Ңв”Җidв”Җв”¬в”Җd1в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җd2в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
1. в”Ӯ 1  в”Ӯ id1-1-d1 в”Ӯ id2-1-d2 в”Ӯ
2. в”Ӯ 4  в”Ӯ id1-2-d1 в”Ӯ id2-2-d2 в”Ӯ
3. в”Ӯ 2  в”Ӯ id1-2-d1 в”Ӯ id2-2-d2 в”Ӯ
4. в”Ӯ 3  в”Ӯ id1-1-d1 в”Ӯ id2-1-d2 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
select 2 (order by):
```
в”Ңв”Җidв”Җв”¬в”Җd1в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җd2в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
1. в”Ӯ 1  в”Ӯ id2-1-d2 в”Ӯ id1-1-d1 в”Ӯ
2. в”Ӯ 3  в”Ӯ id2-1-d2 в”Ӯ id1-1-d1 в”Ӯ
3. в”Ӯ 4  в”Ӯ id2-2-d2 в”Ӯ id1-2-d1 в”Ӯ
4. в”Ӯ 2  в”Ӯ id2-2-d2 в”Ӯ id1-2-d1 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
In column d1 data from column d2 and vice versa.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_fuzz.t_local (id Int32, c1 VARCHAR(255), c2 VARCHAR(255), d1 VARCHAR(255), d2 VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO test_fuzz.t_local (id, c1, c2, d1, d2) SELECT id, c1, c2, CONCAT(IFNULL(c1, 'NULL'), '-d1'), CONCAT(c2, '-d2') FROM (VALUES (1, 'id1-1', 'id2-1'), (2, NULL, 'id2-2'), (3, 'id1-1', NULL), (4, 'id1-2', 'id2-2')) AS t(id, c1, c2);
CREATE TABLE test_fuzz.t AS SELECT * FROM test_fuzz.t_local;
SELECT id, d1, d2 FROM test_fuzz.t ORDER BY d2;
key_bug_features: ALIAS fields in distributed queries with sorting
